outcome.headline: Agent sank entire 10-minute budget into OCR/pixel-analysis attempts on figure.jpg and timed out without ever writing /app/answer.txt; verifier returned reward 0.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: Presence of /app/answer.txt containing [ANSWER]X[/ANSWER] whose letter maps (via /app/.letter_mapping.json) to the ideal text 'Right '.
outcome.what_agent_produced: No answer file was ever written; agent was still iterating on image analysis when AgentTimeoutError fired at 600s.
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent OCR/pixel-analysis loop on a figure the harness cannot render
failure_modes.description: Across steps 4-24 the agent kept switching tactics (PIL inspect → install tesseract → full-image OCR → panel-slice OCR → saturation thresholding → dark-region detection → legend OCR → rotated-label OCR) without ever obtaining bar heights or x-axis labels, then ran out of budget.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: The OCR is really struggling with the x-axis labels. The labels appear to be very small or angled text that tesseract can't read well. Let me try a completely different approach.

failure_modes.name: No fallback answer written before timeout
failure_modes.description: Agent recognized at step 2 that it could not view the image, yet never wrote any best-guess [ANSWER] to /app/answer.txt as a fallback; when the 600s budget elapsed the verifier had no file to grade and returned 0.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: I can't read the image directly.

counterfactual.X: After step 2 (or at any later point), write a best-guess like `printf '[ANSWER]A[/ANSWER]\n' > /app/answer.txt` so the verifier has a parseable answer even if OCR fails.
counterfactual.Y: Verifier would at least parse a letter and have a 1/5 chance of matching 'Right'; in this trial the canonical mapping was unknown to the agent so this would not guarantee success, but it eliminates the deterministic 0 from a missing file.

reward_hacking.evidence: Agent attempted only legitimate OCR/pixel-analysis on /app/figure.jpg and never touched /app/.letter_mapping.json contents, /tests/, or the verifier path.

issues: The task requires interpreting a scientific figure (bar chart in panel c), but the qwen-coder harness exposes files as text only — no vision modality is wired to the model — so the task is effectively unsolvable without OCR-of-pixels heroics or guessing.
task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier reads /app/.letter_mapping.json and /tests/choices.json which are both outside /app workspace control at agent time; mapping is generated by entrypoint before agent starts. Agent could in principle read /app/.letter_mapping.json plus /tests/choices.json to determine the letter — but /tests is not exposed in the agent workspace, and the agent never attempted this.

notes_for_aggregation: Vision-required MCQ + text-only harness → degenerate OCR loop → AgentTimeoutError with no answer.txt. Pattern: image-modality tasks under qwen-coder waste full budget without delivering.
